metric_value: Sorted 10,000 receipts per night at a family restaurant in Chatham, MA; described as 'the founding frustration'.
notes: Chatham, MA family restaurant. Period: 1960-1965.

metric_name: Cornell University enrollment; Hotel Ezra Cornell punch-card failure
metric_value: Kastner attended Cornell 1965-1966; experienced a punch-card failure at Hotel Ezra Cornell that led to his MIT decision.
Operated IBM 7094 and 360/65; encountered ELIZA; self-taught FORTRAN and assembler. Period: 1966-1967.
Kastner encountered ELIZA during his MIT computer operator period, 1966-1967.
notes: Significant as early AI exposure predating later AI advisory work.

metric_value: Kastner self-taught FORTRAN as MIT computer operator, 1966-1967.
Kastner self-taught IBM assembler alongside FORTRAN at MIT, 1966-1967.
Ran 360/65 SysGen, CHASE simulation, and acquired first paying clients at Cornell, 1967-1968.
$360/hour CPU time billed at PHI service bureau.

metric_value: Kastner served as applications programmer at PHI, Arlington MA, 1969-1973.

metric_name: PHI client: Cumberland Farms systems work

metric_name: PHI client: First Chicago DDA system
metric_value: Kastner worked on demand deposit accounting (DDA) system for First National Bank of Chicago at PHI.

notes: An early instance of Y2K remediation, decades before it became industry-wide issue.

metric_value: Kastner involved in Cosmos/CAS code generator development at PHI, 1969-1973.

metric_value: Kastner served as senior consultant at Arthur D. Little Systems, Cambridge MA, 1973-1979.

notes: One of the largest IT migrations of the era.

metric_name: ADL projects: Rio hospital; police systems
metric_value: ADL projects included a Rio (Brazil) hospital system and police systems during 1973-1979.
Kastner's ADL portfolio included design projects for paper mills, 1973-1979.
Kastner rescued a troubled IT project for Houghton Mifflin while at Arthur D. Little.

metric_name: Kastner joins Prime Computer as Market Planning executive
metric_value: Joined Prime Computer, Natick MA, as Market Planning executive, 1979-1981.
Prime Computer was the #1 NYSE stock in 1980 with a +272% gain.
notes: Extraordinary stock performance reflecting minicomputer sector boom.

metric_name: Commercial vertical markets and 4GL emergence at Prime
metric_value: Kastner focused on commercial vertical markets and witnessed 4GL emergence at Prime Computer, 1979-1981.

metric_name: Kastner joins Stratus Computer as Marketing executive
metric_value: Joined Stratus Computer, Marlborough MA, as Marketing executive, 1981-1987.
Stratus's Pair-and-Spare architecture was the core technical differentiator during Kastner's 1981-1987 tenure.
Stratus produced the IBM OEM System 88 during Kastner's tenure; a major OEM arrangement.
Kastner experienced competitive 'fault-tolerant wars' between Stratus and Tandem during 1981-1987.
Kastner was involved in the XA 2000 product launch at Stratus Computer.
John Morgridge was a key executive colleague during Kastner's time at Stratus Computer (1981-1987).
notes: Morgridge later became Cisco CEO.

metric_name: Kastner joins DEC as Senior Marketing Executive
metric_value: Joined Digital Equipment Corporation, Maynard MA, as Senior Marketing Executive, 1987-1988.
Kastner led the DECtp launch at DEC; described as 'most important launch after VAX'.
DEC conducted a secret (blind) benchmark vs. IBM 3090 under Kastner's marketing leadership; IBM responded with DB2 v2.

notes: SNA Tax discovered during benchmark.

metric_value: Kastner committed DEC as the first TPC member in August 1988 over dinner with Omri Serlin.

metric_value: Kastner co-founded Aberdeen Group, Boston MA, as Chief Research Officer; partnership founding model with cooperative revenue.

metric_value: 4 partners at founding in 1990.

metric_value: ~12 analysts by 1993; partnership model at scale.

notes: Venture capital idea considered at this point.

metric_value: $9.4M revenue; net loss ($552K); ~60 analysts. Post-VC expansion begins.

metric_value: $14.1M revenue; net profit $732K; ~90 analysts. Internet boom acceleration.

metric_name: Aberdeen revenue and headcount 2001; NASDAQ crash impact
metric_value: $14.9M revenue; net loss ($3.2M); ~150 analysts (max headcount). NASDAQ crash effect.
notes: Paradoxically hit peak headcount while revenues fell sharply.

metric_value: $9.4M revenue; net loss ($1.3M); ~80 analysts. Crash consolidation underway.
notes: Audited by Ernst & Young.

metric_value: $6.5M revenue; net loss ($3.1M); ~40 analysts. Post-crash trough.
$5.1M revenue; net loss ($2.0M); ~30 analysts. Revenue trough.
notes: Lowest revenue year.

metric_value: $8.7M revenue; net profit $868K; ~19 analysts. Recovery; profitability restored.
$10.8M revenue (Jan-Aug only); net profit $3.97M; ~19 analysts. Acquired by Harte-Hanks in October 2006.
notes: Audited by BDO Seidman.

metric_name: Kastner ghostwrites Yankee Group OLTP paper
metric_value: Kastner ghostwrote 'The Future of Transaction Processing' for Yankee Group in 1987; predicted microprocessor OLTP dominance.
notes: Proved prescient; formed intellectual foundation for TPC co-founding.

metric_value: Kastner predicted in 1987 that microprocessors would come to dominate OLTP workloads, displacing mainframes.
notes: Prediction proved correct.

metric_name: DEC blind benchmark vs IBM 3090; SNA Tax discovered
metric_value: DECtp blind benchmark vs. IBM 3090 conducted 1988; 'SNA Tax' overhead quantified.

metric_name: DEC announces benchmark results; IBM DB2 v2 response
metric_value: June 1988: DEC announces benchmark results. IBM responds with DB2 v2 release in October 1988.
August 1988: Kastner committed DEC as first TPC member to Omri Serlin over dinner.
August 8, 1988: DEC TPC leadership leaked to MIS Week; TPC publicly launched.
1989: TPC-A (debit-credit) standard published with first audited results.
1990: TPC-B benchmark standard published.
1992: TPC-C (order-entry) published; described as 'the definitive OLTP benchmark'.

metric_name: Kastner serves as independent TPC benchmark auditor
metric_value: 1992-1995: Kastner served as independent TPC benchmark auditor for Stratus, IBM, and Groupe Bull.

metric_name: Groupe Bull as TPC benchmark audit client
metric_value: Groupe Bull was one of Kastner's TPC benchmark audit clients, 1992-1995.

metric_name: TPC requires certified auditors; Kastner exits auditing
metric_value: Post-1995: TPC required certified auditors; Kastner exited auditing practice.
TPC continues publishing benchmarks including TPC-H, TPC-DS, and TPC-E across the industry.
